name: Cantonese
notes: Cantonese (粵語), Yue Chinese, Guangdong/Hong Kong/Macau. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified and none is claimed. Cantonese is written with Chinese characters (written Cantonese adds Cantonese-specific characters); the script is logographic, so there is no letter-to-sound mapping — a romanisation (Jyutping, or Yale) would be required as input, exactly as `zh` requires pinyin. Cantonese has six contrastive tones, which Jyutping writes as trailing digits.
